raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California lawmakers announced legislation to reform the Employment Development Department's (EDD) unemployment insurance program.",
    "There have been widespread reports of issues at the EDD since the start of the pandemic, causing difficulties for millions of Californians in accessing benefits.",
    "EDD has fallen prey to rampant unemployment fraud, particularly from California prisons.",
    "Multiple audits have concluded that problems at the EDD have persisted for decades with little effort to address them.",
    "The California governor established a strike team that produced actionable recommendations for improving the EDD.",
    "EDD was overwhelmed and unprepared for the crisis brought on by the pandemic.",
    "California is one of three states that does not currently offer a direct deposit option for unemployment benefits.",
    "The lack of language access at EDD presents significant barriers for claimants who do not speak English.",
    "Assembly Bill 401 aims to ensure timely language support for all Californians seeking EDD services.",
    "Assembly Bill 110 requires the EDD to cross-check incarceration records to prevent fraudulent benefit payments.",
    "Assembly Bill 402 will establish an Office of the Claimant Advocate within the EDD to protect claimants' rights.",
    "Assembly Bill 397 aims to allow claimants to correct mistaken answers on certifications without losing benefits.",
    "Legislative oversight hearings have revealed numerous issues that need correction at the EDD."
  ]
}
```
